name: alpha-assistant
changelog: Your assistant now starts knowing the team — the shared brain is pre-loaded from our existing Notion: every teammate from the directory, our foundational Operating Principles, and the projects with recent activity
From your first session, 'who owns X?' and 'what did we decide about Y?' get precise answers — no warm-up period, no empty databases, no 'give me a few weeks'

The Notion hub is now radically concise — just the download link and the shared brain. Everything else (Contract, onboarding, promotion rules, roadmap, design decisions) lives inside the folder, one canonical copy, no duplication
No action required if you're already on v1.4 — run /update to pick up the new pack; your assistant will just know more from its next session
changelog_semver: Added packs/company-brain-seed.md — documented one-time bulk seeding procedure for the brain. Maintainer-run, dedup-safe on re-run. Seeds People (all, high-confidence) + foundational Decisions (curated from Operating Framework) + active Projects (recent activity, curated). Insights intentionally empty.

Notion brain seeded: all active People rows from Team directory, 5 foundational Decisions from Operating Principles, 5 active Projects from last-2-weeks activity (Alpha Turbo Squad, Coaching Summer Program, Summer Learning Plan, Pilot 2 Launch, April Functional Priorities).

Notion hub radically slimmed: former sub-pages (Overview, Get Your Assistant, AI Contract, Folder Template, Tool Setup, Promotion Rules, Governance, Roadmap, Onboarding Modules, Packs Library, Design Decisions) archived. Single source of truth for those docs now lives inside the folder.
packs/README.md: added company-brain-seed.md entry to recommended first packs and MCP matrix.
Schema quirk documented in seed pack: Projects.Source options do not include 'document' (only conversation/meeting/manual/decision) — seed uses 'manual'. Decisions.Source does include 'document'.

download._note: sha256 + size are populated in the release-attached copy of this manifest. The in-repo copy intentionally omits them because its own hash would be a self-reference. Assistants read the remote manifest via manifest_url to get the live numbers.
